By my math 17 seats should be $169.83, and even adding part-month proration for the new seats I can't get to $171.27 exactly. Probably there's a proration formula I'm not seeing, but accounting bounces this back to me every single time the numbers don't tie out, and I genuinely cannot face another round of that this week. input: We're running a technical evaluation of Taskloop's REST API ahead of a possible Business-tier purchase (160+ seats). input: During our proof-of-concept we've observed that webhook deliveries from Taskloop are intermittently failing to arrive — roughly 4% of task.updated events never reach our endpoint, with no correlation to anything on our side (endpoint at 100% uptime, returns 200 in under 80ms). Questions: 1) Does Taskloop retry failed webhook deliveries, with what backoff and for how long? 2) Is there a delivery log or replay mechanism we can inspect? 3) Are events guaranteed at-least-once, or best-effort? If it's best-effort with no replay, we'll need to design a polling fallback against the REST API, which materially affects our integration estimate. Documentation on this is thin.
expected: {"category": "data_integration", "urgency": "normal", "summary": "Prospect reports intermittent webhook delivery loss; asks about retries and replay."}
predicted: {"category": "data_integration", "urgency": "normal", "summary": "Webhook failures intermittent; no retry/backoff details or delivery logs available"}

input:
Subject: Webhook redelivery for events dropped during Monday's outage

I read the postmortem your team published last night — appreciated the detail on the queue migration going sideways. I run the overnight ops desk for a logistics company and our escalation tooling is driven entirely by Taskloop webhooks: a task moving into our 'Escalate' column fires a webhook that pages whoever is on call.

During the outage window (roughly 02:00–08:00 UTC per your timeline) we received nothing, and we've confirmed at least 40 task transitions in that window that never produced a delivery. The postmortem says queued events were 'flushed,' but it says nothing about whether events that failed before ever reaching the queue will be replayed.

Two questions: 1) Is there a way to request redelivery for a specific time window? 2) If not, can you export the event log for our workspace for that window so we can reconcile manually? We got lucky this time — nothing critical was missed — but I need to close this gap in our internal incident review.
expected: {"category": "data_integration", "urgency": "normal", "summary": "Asks for redelivery or event log of webhooks dropped during outage window."}
predicted: {"category": "data_integration", "urgency": "normal", "summary": "Request for webhook redelivery or event log export during Monday's outage window"}

input:
Honestly, Taskloop has been the best move we made this year — I migrated all five of my client workspaces over from spreadsheets and the board templates alone save me hours on every onboarding.

So consider this a compliment sandwich: the Slack integration for one workspace (just one of the five) silently disconnects every few days.
